feed.title: Journal of Historical Sociolinguistics
feed.subtitle: Latest articles from Journal of Historical Sociolinguistics (De Gruyter)

entry.title: Lingala only? A history of the D. R. Congo’s ideologies and counter-ideologies of monolingualism
entry.summary: Authors: Margot Luyckfasseel, Michael Meeuwis, Joshua Castillo DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/jhsl-2024-0034 Pages: Page range: 1-31

entry.title: Individual variation and linguistic choices in new dialect formation processes: a case study from Narvik
entry.summary: Authors: Randi Neteland DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/jhsl-2024-0019 Pages: Page range: 79-102

entry.title: The social meaning of the third-person singular -thinflection in Early Modern English sermons
entry.summary: Authors: Hiroshi Yadomi DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/jhsl-2024-0055 Pages: Page range: 103-140

entry.title: Sanskrit, Classical Arabic, Latin and now English – a case of a special kind of lingua franca use and status

entry.summary: Authors: Karoline Kühl, Joshua R. Brown DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/jhsl-2026-0014 Pages: Page range: 179-184
